Italy.Bachelor (Laurea Triennale): High School Diploma with 12 years of schooling. If less than 12 years, foundation year or university credits required. Entrance exam (TOLC) often required.
Italy.Master (Laurea Magistrale): Bachelor's degree in a related field. Minimum GPA requirements vary. English or Italian language proficiency (B2).
Italy.Medicine (Laurea a Ciclo Unico): IMAT exam for English medical schools or Italian test for Italian schools. Highly competitive.

Germany.Bachelor: Abitur equivalent. Foreign students may need Studienkolleg (preparatory course) if their diploma is not recognized. B2/C1 German usually required.
Germany.Master: Bachelor's degree with specific ECTS in related subjects. Many English-taught programs available.

France.Bachelor (Licence): Baccalauréat equivalent. B2 French usually required. Application via Études en France.
France.Master: Licence or equivalent Bachelor's. Strong academic record.

United States.Undergraduate: High school diploma, SAT/ACT (often optional now), TOEFL/IELTS, personal statement.
United States.Graduate: Bachelor's degree, GRE/GMAT (sometimes optional), letters of recommendation, statement of purpose.

United Kingdom.Undergraduate: A-Levels or equivalent (e.g., IB). Application via UCAS. IELTS/PTE required.
United Kingdom.Postgraduate: Upper second-class (2:1) honours degree. Strong references.
